    "redFlags": [
      "Quote is 63% above local average ($970)",
      "Parts type not clearly specified for rear brake pads and rear rotors—only front pads/rotors are branded (Bosch/Brembo aftermarket)",
      "No labor warranty offered despite 12-month parts warranty; typical shops warrant brake labor for 12 months as well",
      "Labor hours not itemized per repair—4 hours total for full brake job (front and rear pads + rotors) appears slightly optimistic; typical book time is 1.5–2 hours per axle",
      "Shop supplies fee of $35 (3.7% of parts) is acceptable but not detailed—lacks transparency on what supplies include"
    ],
    "summary": "Price is fair but slightly elevated. A full brake job (four pads + four rotors) at $1,581.85 total is reasonable for a Honda Civic given the quality parts (Bosch and Brembo are recognized aftermarket brands), fair labor rate ($135/hr is mid-market for independent shops), and parts-only warranty. However, the quote lacks clarity on rear brake component brands and does not extend labor warranty coverage, which is a minor weakness. The 4-hour labor estimate is acceptable for a complete brake system overhaul. Overall, this is a solid quote from an independent shop, though front-loading toward premium aftermarket pads and rotors adds ~15% to cost compared to economy alternatives.",

    },

  "priceBaselineFinding": {
    "severity": "high",
    "category": "price_concern",
    "flag": "Quote is 63% above local average ($970)",
    "detail": "Quote of $1,582 is significantly higher than the local average of $970 across 9 comparable quotes in this area. Note: local average mixes repair scopes, but a gap this large still warrants getting one or two competing estimates.",
    "baseline": "calibration"
  },
  "summary_for_llm": "Total: $1,581.85.\nVehicle: 2018 Honda Civic (87,000 mi).\nLabor rate: $135/hr.\nLabor hours: 4.\nParts total: $935.85.\n\n5 repair item(s):\n  - Front brake pads (Bosch QuietCast) [critical]\n  - Front rotors (Brembo) [critical]\n  - Rear brake pads [critical]\n  - Rear rotors [critical]\n  - Brake system labor (4 hours) [critical]\n\n5 red flag(s):\n  - Quote is 63% above local average ($970)\n  - Parts type not clearly specified for rear brake pads and rear rotors—only front pads/rotors are branded (Bosch/Brembo aftermarket)\n  - No labor warranty offered despite 12-month parts warranty; typical shops warrant brake labor for 12 months as well\n  - Labor hours not itemized per repair—4 hours total for full brake job (front and rear pads + rotors) appears slightly optimistic; typical book time is 1.5–2 hours per axle\n  - Shop supplies fee of $35 (3.7% of parts) is acceptable but not detailed—lacks transparency on what supplies include\n\nSummary: Price is fair but slightly elevated. A full brake job (four pads + four rotors) at $1,581.85 total is reasonable for a Honda Civic given the quality parts (Bosch and Brembo are recognized aftermarket brands), fair labor rate ($135/hr is mid-market for independent shops), and parts-only warranty. However, the quote lacks clarity on rear brake component brands and does not extend labor warranty coverage, which is a minor weakness. The 4-hour labor estimate is acceptable for a complete brake system overhaul. Overall, this is a solid quote from an independent shop, though front-loading toward premium aftermarket pads and rotors adds ~15% to cost compared to economy alternatives."
}
